punt_reason: Section block is 2270 words against target_max 700 (3.24× over). Subtraction profile: sentence_complexity -2 (11 sentences >35 words) + forward_references -10 (10 forward references: 'See Part 5'; 'See Part 5'; 'see Section 2.9'; etc — rubric floor capped). The dominant defect is forward_references, which Categories A/B/C/D/E do NOT address — forward-reference fixes require either (a) inlining the referenced content (which violates RWR-5 'no new claims' since the referenced content currently lives in another section) or (b) replacing the bare reference with a one-sentence preview that explains why the reader should follow it (a Category not in the spec). A Category A sentence-split pass would reduce sentence_complexity from -2 to ~0 but leave forward_references at -10 and length at 1, giving composite ~3.0 (delta +2.6 mechanically clears the gate, but the fix doesn't address the dominant defect).
category_gap_signal: forward_references subtraction (rubric §4) is at floor -10 here and not addressable by current Categories A/B/C/D/E. Either (a) the rubric's forward-references penalty needs a 'fix' category — Category F = 'forward-reference inlining or contextualizing preview' — or (b) forward-references is a structural-architecture issue (sections referencing other sections) that requires operator editorial coordination, not a per-surface rewrite.
recommendation_to_operator: Audit forward references in this block. Each 'See Part 5' / 'see Section 2.9' should either: (1) include a one-sentence preview ('Section 2.9 establishes that the V13 coordinate system is self-referential; see there for the full derivation') so the reader knows why to follow it; or (2) be removed if the reference is decorative (no load-bearing context). A future Phase 2 category for forward-reference fixes might be productive — surface to tinker for design review.
